name: Rhubarb Cream Pie (Monteith)
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cup Rhubarb, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
1/2 cup Half and half or milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Fit pastry into a 9-inch pie pan.
2. Put rhubarb in the pastry-lined pan.
3. In a bowl, beat egg yolks, cream, 2/3 cup sugar, and flour until smooth. Pour mixture over rhubarb.
4. Bake in a 375°F oven on the lowest rack until the pastry is golden brown and the custard appears set in the middle when the pie is jiggled, about 40 to 45 minutes. Cover the edges of the pastry if needed to prevent over-browning.
5. In a large bowl, beat egg whites and cream of tartar at high speed with an electric mixer until frothy.
6. Gradually whip in the remaining sugar and vanilla, beating until the whites hold stiff, glossy peaks (be careful not to over-beat).
7. Pile meringue onto the hot filling.
8. With a spatula, swirl the meringue over the filling and up against the pastry rim.
9. Bake at 400°F for 3 to 5 minutes until the meringue is tinged brown (if concerned about safety, continue past this point until the center of the meringue reaches 160°F, about 7 minutes).
10. Serve warm or at room temperature. If made ahead, let cool, then cover without touching the meringue and chill until the next day. Unfortunately, this pie does not hold well and is best served the day it is made.
